name: Parse an e-invoice into structured fields

parameters.jsCode:
// A minimal EN 16931-valid invoice, the shape an ERP export would map to.
const invoice = {
  number: 'INV-3001',
  issueDate: '2026-01-15',
  dueDate: '2026-02-14',
  currencyCode: 'EUR',
  buyerReference: 'LEITWEG-01',
  seller: { name: 'Seller GmbH', vatId: 'DE123456789', address: { street: 'Hauptstrasse 1', city: 'Berlin', postalCode: '10115', countryCode: 'DE' } },
  buyer: { name: 'Buyer GmbH', vatId: 'DE987654321', address: { street: 'Marktplatz 2', city: 'Munich', postalCode: '80331', countryCode: 'DE' } },
  lines: [{ description: 'Consulting services', quantity: 10, unitCode: 'HUR', unitPrice: 100, lineTotal: 1000, vatRate: 19, vatCategoryCode: 'S' }],
  taxSummary: [{ vatCategoryCode: 'S', vatRate: 19, taxableAmount: 1000, taxAmount: 190 }],
  paymentMeans: { typeCode: '58', iban: 'DE89370400440532013000' },
  totalNetAmount: 1000,
  totalTaxAmount: 190,
  totalGrossAmount: 1190,
};
return [{ json: { invoice } }];

parameters.content:
## Parse an e-invoice into structured fields

1. A sample invoice is generated as an XRechnung so the flow is runnable on its own.
2. beliq parses the document into a structured invoice on the item JSON.
3. A Code node reads the fields a downstream step needs (number, seller, totals).

Swap the first two nodes for a trigger that brings in a real document (a webhook body, a read binary file, an email attachment), then point **beliq: parse** at that binary field.

Parsing is read-only: beliq never sends or files the invoice. Set up your **beliq API** credential, then run it. Start free at https://beliq.eu
